summary_one_line: Input must be a well-formed Set-Cookie HTTP header that parses into a stored cookie, so the jar's per-bucket list is non-empty when remove_expired scans it.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The input must contain a recognizable HTTP response with the literal token 'HTTP/' followed by a 'Set-Cookie:' header whose name=value parses cleanly, so Curl_cookie_add accepts the cookie and links it into cookies->cookies[i]; only then does remove_expired's while(co) loop see a non-NULL list head and take the true side.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds carry the exact ASCII bytes 'HTTP/' at offset 0x24 and 'Set-Coo' at the tail, and W reaches the cookie-storage path (Curl_cookie_add entry-line W hits=20 vs L=0), so the per-bucket cookie list is populated and while(co) is entered (W T=20 on L404).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds carry random havoc bytes (mostly '%','A','+' runs) at the same offsets instead of the 'HTTP/'/'Set-Cookie' literals, never reach Curl_cookie_add (L entry-line hits=0), so the cookie list stays empty and remove_expired only ever takes the false side of while(co) (L T=0).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc's I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver buffer recorded when curl's parser compares header bytes against the literals 'HTTP/' and 'Set-Cookie' and substitutes the matching operand at those input offsets, manufacturing the exact tokens that naive/value_profile must instead stumble onto by chance; both decisive pairs differ only by I2S, and the winning cmplog seed's ancestor chain contains I2S-floor dash rows.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the while(co) loop head in remove_expired and the globally blocked side is the true branch.
Both decisive pairs are I2S deltas with prob_div 1.0, winner resolving 10/10 and loser blocked 10/10.
The second decisive pair is value_profile_cmplog over value_profile, also an I2S delta.
The true side of while(co) requires a non-NULL cookie list head taken from cookies->cookies[i].
Winner seeds reach the cookie-storage function while losers never do, isolating the storage path as the gating condition.
Winner inputs contain the literal 'HTTP/' token at the same offset where loser inputs hold random bytes.
Winner seed bytes show 'GHTTP/' and 'Set-Coo' literal runs that the loser seeds lack.
Loser seeds are dominated by havoc '%' and 'A' byte runs rather than HTTP/cookie tokens.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes a CMP operand at the matching input offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, so the loser arm of pair 2 cannot perform operand substitution.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 42 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 2b428b2993f47feb`; the 16-level ancestor chain reports I2S-floor signal with 2 dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depth 14 and 15, the under-build floor evidence of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find in cmplog.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where cmplog still resolves the while(co) true side at the same rate as naive after the 'HTTP/' and 'Set-Cookie' literals are replaced with runtime-derived (non-constant) tokens that I2S cannot pre-image.

weakest_evidence_point: The mapping from specific byte-diff offsets (e.g. 0x24 'HTTP/') to the exact CMP that I2S solves is inferred from source plus byte runs, not directly observed in the CmpLogObserver buffer.
